mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% in Aug 2010 suggests ongoing labor market slack
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with declining unemployment trend
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363.7B in Aug 2010 reflects consumer spending patterns
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth rate (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate falls below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable or declining, the real interest rate becomes deeply negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% in Aug 2010 indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with contained inflation
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.2 in Aug 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery in manufacturing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances show positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with expanding retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based demand acceleration across residential and consumer sectors, which drives equity valuations higher over 20 trading days through multiplier effects.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average > prior 6-month average AND RSAFS 3-month average > prior 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 546K units in Jul 2010 shows housing recovery
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 62% of cases, or Kendall tau correlation between joint signal and returns is < 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) exceeding 200K monthly additions signals strong labor demand and rising household income, which supports equity valuations through higher expected corporate revenues and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.3M in Aug 2010 reflects ongoing employment recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-payroll-growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates while the federal funds rate remains near zero, equity investors perceive increased recession risk and demand higher risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13191.5B in Q2 2010 shows post-crisis recovery slowing
regime_hint: weak recovery with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over 3 months combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating economic momentum and profit recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average < prior 6-month average AND UNRATE 1-month change > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.2 and UNRATE at 9.6% in Aug 2010 reflect fragile recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 52% of cases, or Spearman rank correlation between joint signal and returns is > -0.05.
